channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Here are the Big Tech CEOs attending Trump's inauguration
item.description: The CEOs of several of the world’s biggest technology companies are planning to attend President-elect Trump’s inauguration Monday.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Schumer pushes for TikTok ban delay as deadline looms
item.description: Senate Majority Leader Chuck Schumer (D-N.Y.) on Thursday called for a looming TikTok ban to be delayed to give the social media company more time to find an American buyer. "We aren’t against TikTok. We want TikTok to keep going.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Execs are prioritizing skills over degrees
item.description: When considering new hires, 80% of corporate executives will prioritize skills over degrees, with half planning to increase freelance hiring this year to fill in for a gap in AI and other skills, according to a new study from freelancing platform Upwork.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: TikTok users are flocking to alternative apps like RedNote
item.description: TikTok is still three days away from a likely ban in the United States, but many users are already bidding the app farewell and seeking out alternatives.
item.dc:creator: CNN.com Wire Service, The Mercury News

item.title: How agentic AI transforms enterprise IT work
item.description: Technology innovations attract significant interest among CIOs, CTOs, and technology leaders, who are always looking for ways to improve business results with the use of innovative and transformational technologies.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: If the iPhone 17 gets this rumored Face ID upgrade, I'll never call Apple unoriginal again
item.description: A new patent suggests Apple is working on fitting Face ID underneath the iPhone display – but there are still some challenges to overcome.
item.dc:creator: jamie.richards@futurenet.com (Jamie Richards), TechRadar

item.title: Snowflake Open-Sources SwiftKV for Reduced Generative AI Costs
item.description: Snowflake has open-sourced its proprietary approach, SwiftKV, designed to reduce the cost of inference workloads for enterprises running generative AI-based applications.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Nvidia’s new next-gen GPU benchmarks cause concern among PC gamers, particularly with the RTX 5080 – but don’t panic yet
item.description: Not convinced RTX 5000 is a winner? Folks worried about a reliance on ‘fake frames’ may not be that impressed with these fresh benchmarks.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: If Microsoft's Windows 11 24H2 issues continue like this, I'll fully convert to SteamOS for gaming as Ubisoft is forced to fix Assassin’s Creed games
item.description: Windows 11 24H2 has continuously troubled gamers on PC, and ultimately forced Ubisoft's hand to provide a compatibility patch for some of its Assassin's Creed games.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Network convergence will drive enterprise 6G wireless strategies
item.description: The traditional separation between Wi-Fi and cellular networks — a cornerstone of enterprise wireless planning — is facing fundamental challenges according to a new 6G vision statement released this week by the World Broadband Association, “It is important that the 6G era brings together cellular, W
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Biden executive order aims to shore up US cyber defenses
item.description: An executive order issued by President Joe Biden just days before he leaves office aims to shore up America's cyber defenses while making it easier to go after foreign countries that launch cyberattacks.
item.dc:creator: David Klepper, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump adviser says president-elect is exploring options to 'preserve' TikTok
item.description: Trump’s pick for national security adviser, Florida Rep. Mike Waltz, said during an interview on Wednesday that the president-elect is exploring options to “preserve” TikTok.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Stanford AI expert’s credibility shattered by fake, AI-created sources: judge
item.description: Hancock, a prolific, high-profile researcher whose work has received some $20 million in grant support over the past two decades, charged $600 an hour to prepare the testimony the judge tossed.
item.dc:creator: Ethan Baron, The Mercury News

item.title: What to know about RedNote, a TikTok alternative
item.description: TikTok patrons are turning to a new platform in light of the nationwide ban set to shut down the app on Sunday. RedNote, a Chinese short-form video platform, is gaining ground as users look for an alternative to the social media company owned by the China's ByteDance.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Fox will stream next month's Super Bowl for free on Tubi
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Fox will stream the Super Bowl on Tubi for the first time, giving streamers and cord cutters a chance to watch the big game on Feb. 9 for free. Tubi was acquired by Fox Corporation in 2020. It is a free, ad-supported streaming service.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Google Partners with AP to Deliver News Through AI Chatbot Gemini
item.description: Google's AI chatbot Gemini will feature up-to-date news from The Associated Press, marking the tech giant's first such deal with a news publisher.
item.dc:creator: Matt O'Brien, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Qualcomm purloins Intel’s chief Xeon designer with eyes toward data center development
item.description: If Intel was hoping for a turnaround in 2025, it will have to wait at least a little bit longer. The chief architect for Intel’s Xeon server processors has defected to chip rival Qualcomm, which is making yet another run at entering the data center market.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Microsoft creates new internal unit for AI development
item.description: Microsoft is creating a new engineering group focused on artificial intelligence: Core AI — Platform and Tools. The group combines the company’s Developer Division with the AI Platforms work teams and some employees who previously reported to Microsoft’s CTO.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Microsoft adds ‘pay-as-you-go’ access for AI agents with M365 Copilot Chat
item.description: Microsoft has added a “pay-as-you-go” option to access AI agents alongside its free M365 Copilot Chat plan.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: What the tech industry wants from the Trump administration and Congress
item.description: The technology industry is calling on the incoming Trump administration and Congress to create the necessary tax and regulatory environment. The post What the tech industry wants from the Trump administration and Congress first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Tom Temin, Federal News Network

item.title: Why Lenovo keeps making weird laptops
item.description: Of all the laptops that debut during the CES trade show every January, Lenovo’s tend to be the most interesting. For years now, the company has indulged in public experimentation, launching laptops with foldable displays, dual screens, and secondary e-ink surfaces.
item.dc:creator: Jared Newman, Fast Company

item.title: Why you should use Docker and OCI containers
item.description: A book published in 1981, called Nailing Jelly to a Tree, describes software as “nebulous and difficult to get a firm grip on.” That was true in 1981, and it is no less true four decades later.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: New Brazil law restricts use of smartphones in elementary and high schools
item.description: SAO PAULO (AP) — Brazil's President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva on Monday signed a bill restricting the use of smartphones at school, following a global trend for such limitations. The move will impact students at elementary and high schools across the South American nation starting in February.
item.dc:creator: MAURICIO SAVARESE and DIARLEI RODRIGUES, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 5-minute cleanup for your phone and computer
item.description: Technology expert Kim Komando shares her easy tips on how to declutter your phones, computers and devices from the files you no longer need.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: CISA publishes security goals for software development process, product design
item.description: The US Cybersecurity & Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) has published IT sector-specific goals (IT SSGs) to protect against cyber threats, including 11 software development process goals and seven product design goals.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: 800,000 VW electric vehicle owners' data exposed by software bug
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson has the details on a Volkswagen data leak that exposed the personal data of around 800,000 EV owners.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Red Hat completes Neural Magic acquisition
item.description: Jan. 13, 2025 update: Red Hat announced it has completed its acquisition of Neural Magic, a pioneer in software and algorithms that accelerate gen AI inference workloads. Red Hat first announced its intent to acquire the company on Nov. 12, 2024. Today’s announcement confirms the close of the deal.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: AI Researchers Predict Diminishing Returns on Generative AI in 2025
item.description: A trio of AI researchers and experts predict that companies and investors will have to lower their expectations regarding what generative AI can do in 2025 due to scalability, regulation, and sustainability issues.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Britain's leader unveils AI strategy, including vow to rip up red tape hindering AI projects
item.description: LONDON (AP) — The British government pledged on Monday to rip up the red tape hindering construction of data centers that underpin artificial intelligence while promoting its “pro-innovation” approach to regulating the technology.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: CISA aims to help cyber, tech pros find their path
item.description: CISA’s National Initiative for Cybersecurity Careers and Studies helps cyber pros keep up with technology changes ranging from AI to zero trust. The post CISA aims to help cyber, tech pros find their path first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: China’s newest humanoid robot is ready to serve like never before
item.description: A Chinese company has developed a new bipedal humanoid robot that stands over 5 feet tall and can tackle a wide range of tasks in different settings.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Fox News AI Newsletter: Tech leaders' message to Biden
item.description: Stay up to date on the latest AI technology advancements and learn about the challenges and opportunities AI presents now and for the future.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: AI isn’t going anywhere: Prompts to make life easier
item.description: Artificial intelligence can be a helpful tool for anyone who is willing to give it a try; here are some simple prompts you can use in order to make your life easier.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Prime Video's use of AI, Next Gen Stats on NFL games is helping viewers understand the game better
item.description: CULVER CITY, Calif. (AP) — The Los Angeles Rams defense appeared to be content to drop back in coverage and prevent San Francisco’s Brock Purdy from completing a Hail Mary on the final play of their Thursday night game on Dec. 12. The broadcast on Amazon Prime Video saw it differently, though.
item.dc:creator: JOE REEDY, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: TikTok gets frosty reception at Supreme Court in fight to stave off ban
item.description: TikTok received a frosty reception in its fight to save the platform at the Supreme Court, which during oral arguments Friday expressed sympathy with the government’s national security concerns about the platform’s ties to China.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, NewsNation

item.title: The top five procurement themes for 2025
item.description: 2025 will see continued focus on supply chain resiliency, often revolving around support for and investment in the domestic industrial base. The post The top five procurement themes for 2025 first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Roger Waldron, Federal News Network

item.title: Chinese auto giant wants to make flying cars your next commute option
item.description: An automotive maker in China has developed a flying car that is setting the stage for a new era in how we think about commuting and connectivity in cities.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Supreme Court seems likely to uphold Jan. 19 TikTok shutdown
item.description: The case asks the high court to weigh the First Amendment against serious national security concerns.
item.dc:creator: Tom Dempsey, NewsNation

item.title: Cohere goes ‘North’ with agentic AI
item.description: Large language model (LLM) provider Cohere has unveiled its new agentic AI offering — North — a low-code platform that will allow enterprises to build and deploy agents across different business functions.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: OPM giving two big data platforms a much-needed facelift
item.description: Taka Ariga, the chief data officer at the Office of Personnel Management, said the agency is modernizing, better integrating the EHRI and FedScope databases. The post OPM giving two big data platforms a much-needed facelift first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network
